title: Spicy Dense Syrian Dried White Corn Stir-fry
description: A low-sodium-friendly brunch dish featuring spicy and dense flavors from dried white corn in a savory stir-fry sauce.

ingredients.name: vinegar
ingredients.notes: rice vinegar or apple cider vinegar works well

ingredients.name: minced ginger
ingredients.notes: optional, for added depth of flavor

ingredients.name: salt
ingredients.notes: lightly sprinkled on corn before cooking

instructions: Preheat a wok or frying pan over medium-high heat. Add olive oil.
Sprinkle lightly salted dried white corn and minced garlic into the hot pan, tossing to coat evenly with oil. Cook for about 3-5 minutes until corn is slightly golden brown but not burnt.
Add chili flakes and optional ginger if using. Stir well to combine and cook for another minute or so.
Drizzle in soy sauce and vinegar; stir gently to mix all ingredients thoroughly.
Reduce heat to low-medium, cover the pan, and let it simmer for about 2-3 minutes to meld flavors together. Keep an eye on it as corn can easily overcook quickly under these conditions.
Turn off heat immediately after the mixture comes to a gentle simmer; this will prevent the dish from becoming too soupy or dry.
Serve hot, garnished with additional fresh herbs if desired.
